tools.description: R packages with the data-generating processes and simulated datasets (with known ground-truth effects) from the 2016/2017 Atlantic Causal Inference Conference competitions.
tools.name: ACIC Competition data (aciccomp)

tools.description: Curated collection of reference Bayesian networks (ASIA, ALARM, HEPAR2, etc.) with known ground-truth structure in multiple formats, the standard ground-truth benchmark for structure-learning evaluation.
tools.name: bnlearn Bayesian Network Repository

tools.description: GSK benchmark suite (with curated single-cell perturbation datasets) for evaluating network/causal-graph inference methods from gene-perturbation data.
tools.name: CausalBench

tools.description: R/Python/Stata packages providing the example datasets (LaLonde, NSW, etc.) used in The Effect, Causal Inference: The Mixtape, and What If textbooks.
tools.name: causaldata

tools.description: Reference repo bundling the widely-cited IHDP (Infant Health and Development Program) semi-synthetic benchmark CSVs with known ground-truth treatment effects used across ITE papers.
tools.name: CEVAE datasets (IHDP)

tools.description: Python framework providing standard causal-inference benchmark datasets (IHDP, IBM ACIC) plus synthetic-data generation and baseline comparison for evaluating ITE methods.
tools.name: JustCause

tools.description: Realistic causal-inference benchmark that fits generative models to real data (LaLonde PSID/CPS, Twins) to produce samples with known ground-truth treatment effects.
tools.name: RealCause

tools.description: Standard benchmark of ~100 bivariate cause-effect pairs with ground-truth causal direction for evaluating pairwise causal-discovery methods (Mooij et al. 2016).
tools.name: Tübingen Cause-Effect Pairs

tools.description: Python sandbox of dynamic simulators with known ground-truth causal effects for stress-testing causal-inference and sequential decision-making methods.
tools.name: WhyNot

tools.description: Amortized variational inference for causal structure learning (NeurIPS 2022), predicting causal graphs directly from data via a trained neural network.
tools.name: AVICI

tools.description: Snakemake workflow to run, develop and benchmark causal-discovery/structure-learning algorithms across many libraries (bnlearn, pcalg, causal-learn, gCastle, Tetrad, etc.) with data generators and metrics.
tools.name: benchpress

tools.description: Independent Python package (built on pgmpy) for Bayesian network structure learning, parameter learning, inference and sampling.
tools.name: bnlearn (Python)

tools.description: Widely used R package for Bayesian network structure learning (constraint-based, score-based, hybrid), parameter learning and inference.
tools.name: bnlearn (R)

tools.description: Python package for graph and pairwise causal discovery, bridging to R packages (pcalg, bnlearn) and providing deep-learning-based methods.
tools.name: Causal Discovery Toolbox (CDT)

tools.description: Command-line interface wrapping the Tetrad causal-discovery algorithms for running searches on data files from a shell.
tools.name: causal-cmd

tools.description: Comprehensive Python library of classic and state-of-the-art causal discovery algorithms (PC, FCI, GES, LiNGAM, Granger, etc.) for learning causal structure from observational data.
tools.name: causal-learn

tools.description: Python package for creating, manipulating and learning causal DAGs, including GSP/IGSP permutation-based and interventional structure-learning algorithms.
tools.name: causaldag

tools.description: Python package of baseline causal-discovery algorithms and analytics tools (varsortability, sortnregress) for benchmarking structure learning.
tools.name: CausalDisco

tools.description: Python library for learning Bayesian network structure (NOTEARS-based) and reasoning about causal relationships for decision-making.
tools.name: CausalNex

tools.description: Microsoft's deep-learning library for end-to-end causal discovery and inference, including the DECI amortized causal-discovery model.
tools.name: causica

tools.description: Python package learning DAGs via continuous optimization using an M-matrix log-determinant acyclicity characterization (DAGMA).
tools.name: DAGMA

tools.description: PyWhy's experimental causal discovery package providing constraint-based and other global structure-learning algorithms with a scikit-learn-style API.
tools.name: dodiscover

tools.description: Python causal structure learning toolbox emphasizing gradient-based methods (NOTEARS, GraN-DAG, etc.) plus data simulators and SHD/F1 evaluation metrics.
tools.name: gCastle

tools.description: R package (Group Iterative Multiple Model Estimation) that recovers group- and individual-level directed contemporaneous/lagged network structure from time series via unified SEM search.
tools.name: gimme

tools.description: Python package implementing the LiNGAM family (ICA-LiNGAM, DirectLiNGAM, VAR-LiNGAM, RCD, etc.) for causal discovery in linear non-Gaussian models.
tools.name: LiNGAM

tools.description: Reference implementation of NO TEARS, casting DAG structure learning as a continuous optimization with a smooth acyclicity constraint.
tools.name: NOTEARS

tools.description: Canonical R package for graphical-model causal structure learning (PC, FCI, RFCI, GIES) and causal effect estimation (IDA).
tools.name: pcalg

tools.description: Python toolkit for probabilistic graphical models with Bayesian network structure learning (PC, Hill-Climb, etc.), parameter learning, inference and causal reasoning.
tools.name: pgmpy

tools.description: Python interface (via JPype) exposing the Java Tetrad causal-discovery algorithms in Python workflows.
tools.name: py-tetrad

tools.description: C++/Python library for probabilistic graphical models (Bayesian networks) with structure learning and causal do-calculus support.
tools.name: pyAgrum / aGrUM

tools.description: NetworkX-compliant causal graph data structures (ADMG, PAG, CPDAG) underpinning the PyWhy causal-discovery ecosystem.
tools.name: pywhy-graphs

tools.description: Long-running Java toolkit and GUI for causal discovery and graphical-causal-model search, the reference implementation of many constraint- and score-based algorithms.
tools.name: Tetrad

tools.description: Python package for causal discovery in time series via the PCMCI/PCMCI+/LPCMCI family of conditional-independence-based algorithms.
tools.name: tigramite

tools.description: Reference implementation of causal discovery with typed directed acyclic graphs, integrating variable-type knowledge into structure learning.
tools.name: typed-DAG (t-DAG)

tools.description: Python package for causal inference using graphical models (DAGs, ADMGs, chain graphs) supporting nonparametric identification and semiparametric estimation under unmeasured confounding.
tools.name: Ananke

tools.description: R package for causal inference using Bayesian Additive Regression Trees (BART), fitting response/treatment models to estimate ATE/ATT/ITE.
tools.name: bartCause

tools.description: Reference R implementation of Bayesian Causal Forests (Hahn-Murray-Carvalho) for heterogeneous treatment-effect estimation with regularized treatment-effect priors.
tools.name: bcf

tools.description: sklearn-style JAX/PyTorch implementations of neural-network CATE estimators including TARNet, CFRNet, DragonNet, SNet, FlexTENet, and NN meta-learners.
tools.name: CATENets

tools.description: Python package for estimating causal dose-response curves (continuous-treatment effects) from observational data with confidence intervals.
tools.name: causal-curve

tools.description: Google's R package estimating the causal effect of an intervention on a time series using a Bayesian structural time-series counterfactual model.
tools.name: CausalImpact

tools.description: Pure-Python port of Google's CausalImpact (statsmodels-based BSTS) for estimating intervention effects on time series.

tools.description: Classic Python package for treatment-effect estimation via propensity-score estimation, trimming, subclassification, matching, weighting, and least-squares.
tools.name: Causalinference

tools.description: IBM's scikit-learn-style package for estimating causal effects from observational data via IPW, standardization, doubly-robust (AIPW), and matching estimators.
tools.name: causallib

tools.description: Uplift modeling package based on the T-learner targeting which customers to treat, usable with both A/B-test and observational data.
tools.name: CausalLift

tools.description: Uber's uplift modeling and causal ML toolkit providing CATE/ITE estimation via S/T/X/R meta-learners, uplift trees/forests, and tree-based treatment selection.
tools.name: CausalML

tools.description: PyMC Labs package for Bayesian (and OLS) causal inference in quasi-experimental designs including synthetic control, interrupted time series, difference-in-differences, regression discontinuity, and instrumental variables.
tools.name: CausalPy

tools.description: R toolbox for heterogeneous treatment effects implementing S/T/X/M/DR meta-learners with honest random forests and BART base learners (now mirrored at forestry-labs/causalToolbox).
tools.name: causalToolbox

tools.description: AutoML library for automated tuning and out-of-sample (energy-score) selection of causal estimators wrapping EconML/DoWhy via FLAML.
tools.name: CausalTune

tools.description: Object-oriented implementation of the double/debiased machine learning framework on top of scikit-learn for partially linear, IV, and interactive regression models.

tools.description: R implementation of the double/debiased machine learning framework built on the mlr3 ecosystem for orthogonal-score estimation of treatment effects.
tools.name: DoubleML (R)

tools.description: End-to-end Python causal inference library that models assumptions as a causal graph and provides a four-step identify/estimate/refute API with refutation-based robustness tests.
tools.name: DoWhy

tools.description: Microsoft ALICE project package for estimating heterogeneous treatment effects (CATE) from observational data using double machine learning, orthogonal/causal forests, DRLearner, DeepIV and meta-learners.
tools.name: EconML

tools.description: Generalized Random Forests for nonparametric heterogeneous treatment-effect estimation (causal forests), including IV, multi-arm, and survival forests with honest confidence intervals.
tools.name: grf

tools.description: R package for longitudinal targeted maximum likelihood estimation (and IPTW/G-computation) of treatment/censoring-specific mean outcomes and marginal structural models.
tools.name: ltmle

tools.description: CRAN R package implementing many summary-data Mendelian randomization methods (IVW, MR-Egger, median, mode, contamination-mixture, cML, debiased IVW) for causal effect estimation.

tools.description: QuantCo's library for CATE estimation with S/T/X/R/DR meta-learners featuring sound cross-fitting, multi-treatment support, and SHAP/optuna integrations.
tools.name: metalearners

tools.description: R package learning optimal shallow decision-tree treatment policies via doubly-robust empirical welfare maximization using grf scores.

tools.description: Wayfair's uplift modeling package implementing the Transformed Outcome method with uplift evaluation/visualization tools (repository archived).
tools.name: pylift

tools.description: scikit-learn-style uplift modeling package providing solo-model/two-model/class-transformation approaches plus uplift metrics and visualizations.
tools.name: scikit-uplift

tools.description: Stochastic tree ensembles (BART/XBART/BCF) in R and Python for supervised learning and Bayesian Causal Forest treatment-effect estimation.

tools.description: Python port of Google's CausalImpact built on TensorFlow Probability for Bayesian structural time-series intervention analysis.
tools.name: tfcausalimpact

tools.description: Susan Gruber & van der Laan's R package for targeted maximum likelihood estimation of ATE/ATT/ATC for a binary point treatment with SuperLearner-based nuisance estimation.
tools.name: tmle (R)

tools.description: Generalized targeted learning (TMLE) framework from the tlverse providing a unified interface for estimating a range of causal target parameters.
tools.name: tmle3

tools.description: R package for two-sample Mendelian randomization using GWAS summary data, interfacing the IEU OpenGWAS database with IVW, MR-Egger, median and mode estimators.
tools.name: TwoSampleMR

tools.description: Booking.com's scalable uplift modeling package with PySpark/H2O implementations of metalearners, uplift random forests, and retrospective/constrained estimation.
tools.name: UpliftML

tools.description: Epidemiology analysis package with causal inference estimators including IPTW/AIPW, g-formula (parametric and Monte Carlo), and TMLE.

tools.description: Applied Econometrics with R companion package providing IV (ivreg), tobit, count and other econometric estimators and datasets.

tools.description: Goodman-Bacon decomposition of two-way fixed-effects DiD estimates into their underlying 2x2 comparison weights.

tools.description: Generates binned scatterplots to visualize conditional means / OLS relationships in Stata.

tools.description: Binscatter least-squares, quantile and GLM regression with valid confidence bands and shape-restriction tests.

tools.description: Cluster-robust (CR2) variance estimators with small-sample corrections and Satterthwaite/Wald hypothesis tests.

tools.description: Plots coefficients/confidence intervals from estimation results or matrices (widely used for event-study graphs).

tools.description: Two-stage difference-in-differences estimator (Gardner 2021) robust to heterogeneous treatment effects under staggered adoption.

tools.description: Entropy balancing reweighting so covariate moments match user-specified targets in observational studies (Hainmueller).

tools.description: Fast design-based OLS/IV estimators (lm_robust, iv_robust, difference_in_means) with robust and cluster-robust standard errors.

tools.description: Produces publication-quality regression tables (esttab/estout) exportable to LaTeX, RTF, HTML and CSV.

tools.description: Extended two-way fixed effects (Wooldridge) DiD via saturated cohort-by-time interactions plus marginal-effects aggregation.

tools.description: Sun & Abraham interaction-weighted event-study estimator robust to heterogeneous treatment effects under staggered timing.

tools.description: Estimates and plots linear panel event-study models following Freyaldenhoven et al., including sup-t bands and pre-trend tests.

tools.description: Counterfactual estimators for causal panel analysis (two-way FE, interactive fixed effects, matrix completion) with diagnostic tests.

tools.description: C-plugin accelerated versions of common Stata data commands (collapse, egen, reshape, pctile) used in large-panel workflows.

tools.description: Publication-quality regression and summary tables (and coefficient plots) for many model classes in multiple output formats.

tools.description: Produces formatted regression-output tables for Word/Excel/LaTeX from Stata estimation results (Roy Wada).

tools.description: Mahalanobis and propensity-score matching with common-support graphing and covariate-imbalance testing (Leuven & Sianesi).
